frontiers.name: derived_structural_pipeline
frontiers.rationale: The mainline is derived from checked-in structural artifacts plus a versioned backend-model bundle instead of stored whole-circuit headline constants.
frontiers.main_risk: Still a backend model below the kickmix ISA boundary, not a primitive-gate lowering.

frontiers.name: isa_liveness_aliasing
frontiers.rationale: Exact ISA-slot liveness implies a lower peak live arithmetic-slot count than the conservative named-slot policy used by the default model.
frontiers.main_risk: This is a backend register-allocation hypothesis, not a theorem-proved primitive mapping.

frontiers.name: signed_lookup_folding_contract
frontiers.rationale: The signed folded contract is exact and heavily audited, but its backend impact remains bounded because arithmetic dominates the current model.
frontiers.main_risk: Further gains from lookup work alone are limited unless the arithmetic/backend layer also changes.

frontiers.name: cross_call_scheduler_and_flattening
frontiers.rationale: A flatter backend might fuse reductions or reuse state across retained calls, but that would move the repository to a different exactness boundary.
frontiers.main_risk: No checked-in exact artifact currently supports this claim.

frontiers.name: primitive_gate_lowering_and_cancellation
frontiers.rationale: Primitive lowering could reveal cancellation opportunities or hidden expansion penalties that are invisible at the ISA layer.
frontiers.main_risk: This is exactly the layer the repository still does not prove today.

stacking_note: Do not multiply these ranges naively. Some frontiers are already shipped experimental scenarios, others are future-work directions, and several overlap strongly.
author_note: This file is a roadmap around the current derived mainline, not a primitive-gate proof beyond the repository's exact ISA boundary.
